title: Modular housing for an rfid reader and antennas
abstract: An assembly including a housing that supports one or more radio frequency identification antennas in communication with a radio frequency identification reader. The housing is mountable to a substantially planar mounting surface above a floor, The radio frequency antenna(s) are supported by the housing and at least some of the antenna(s) are oriented at an angle oblique relative to the substantially planar mounting surface or the floor. In some forms, there are more than one radio frequency antenna and each of the antennas are oriented in different directions to improve reliability and read area of the antennas. Still further, the housing may be modular in form, supporting various configurations for mounting of the constituent components to one another and the mounting surface.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A modular housing for a radio frequency identification reader in electrical communication with at least one radio frequency identification antenna in which the modular housing is mountable to a substantially planar mounting surface above a floor, the housing comprising: a base having sidewalls defining a volume therein configured to receive the radio frequency identification reader and having a side mountable at the substantially planar mounting surface ; at least one wing coupled to the base in which the at least one wing has an angled wall configured to support of a corresponding radio frequency identification antenna at an angle oblique relative to the substantially planar mounting surface or to the floor. 2. The modular housing of claim 1, wherein the at least one wing is removably coupled to the base. 3. The modular housing of claim 2, further comprising at least slot on one of the base and the at least wing and further comprising at least one tab on the other of the base and the at least one wing wherein the at least one wing is removably coupled to the base by inserting the at least on tab into the at least one slot. 4. The modular housing of claim 1, wherein the base has a mounting opening formed in a sidewall thereof which is configured for mounting an electrical sign. 5. The modular housing of claim 1, wherein the at least one wing defines an interior wing volume having an inner wall surface comprising a set of receiving guides for receiving a corresponding radio frequency identification antenna therein. 6. The modular housing of claim 1, wherein an angle of the angled wall is measured from the substantially planar mounting surface to the angled wall and has a value of 20 and 70 degrees . 7. The modular housing of claim 6, wherein the angle of the angled wall is 45 degrees. 8. The modular housing of claim 1, wherein the base is rectangular shaped and the at least one wing is triangular prism shaped . 9. The modular housing of claim 1, further comprising at least one wire opening between the housing and the at least one wing . 10. An assembly comprising: a housing mountable to a substantially planar mounting surface above a floor; at least one radio frequency identification antenna received in the housing, the at least one radio frequency identification antenna being oriented at an angle oblique relative to the substantially planar mounting surface or the floor; and a radio frequency identification reader in electrical communication with the at least one radio frequency identification antenna, wherein, if there are more than one, each of the radio frequency identification antennas are oriented in different directions. 11. The assembly of claim 10, wherein the housing is a modular housing comprising: a base having sidewalls defining a volume receiving the radio frequency identification reader and having a side mountable at the substantially planar mounting surface; and at least one wing coupled to the base in which the at least one wing has an angled wall configured to support of a corresponding radio frequency identification antenna at an angle oblique relative to the substantially planar mounting surface or the floor. 12. The assembly of claim 11, wherein the at least one wing is removably coupled to the base. 13. The assembly of claim 12, further comprising at least slot on one of the base and the at least one wing and further comprising at least one tab on the other of the base and the at least one wing wherein the at least one wing is removably coupled to the base by inserting the at least one tab into the at least one slot. 14. The assembly of claim 11, wherein the at least one wing defines an interior wing volume having an inner wall surface comprising a set of receiving guides for receiving a corresponding radio frequency identification antenna therein. 15. The assembly of claim 14, wherein the set of receiving guides adheres to the corresponding radio frequency identification antenna therein. 16. The assembly of claim 11, wherein the base is rectangular shaped and the at least one wing is triangular prism shaped . 17. The assembly of claim 11, further comprising at least one wire opening between the housing and the at least one wing. 18. The assembly of claim 10, further comprising an electrical sign and wherein the housing has a mounting opening formed in a sidewall thereof which is configured for mounting an electrical sign. 19. The assembly of claim 10, wherein the angle of the at least one radio frequency identification antenna is measured from the substantially planar mounting surface to the at least one radio frequency identification antenna and has a value of 20 and 70 degrees. 20. The assembly of claim 17, wherein the angle of the at least one radio frequency antenna is 45 degrees.
